title: Melee attack-family classification
principles: Melee behavior identifies explicitly represented melee attack families and must not infer specialization or practical suitability from their presence.
Normal attacks, heavy attacks, slide attacks, slams, and heavy slams must remain separate when the source provides distinct attack records.
An attack family should be identified from explicit record semantics rather than from the mere presence of a generic or default numeric value.
Each family must retain its own attack identifier, mode association, damage components, timing evidence, critical profile, and status profile when supplied.
Slam and heavy-slam families may contain separate direct and radial components, but component delivery must be interpreted outside this concept.
Normal-attack sequence behavior remains undetermined without stance-specific evidence.
The existence of several melee attack families does not establish a mixed profile or determine the weapon's primary job.

normal_attack_defined: An explicit normal-attack record is available for the evaluated melee weapon.
The record does not establish stance sequence behavior, hit count, movement, or forced effects.
heavy_attack_defined: An explicit heavy-attack record is available and remains separate from normal attacks and heavy slams.
Its existence does not establish heavy-attack specialization.
slide_attack_defined: An explicit slide-attack record is available.
Its practical coverage and relevance require additional mechanical evidence.
slam_defined: An explicit normal-slam record is available.
Any direct and radial components must remain separately traceable.
heavy_slam_defined: An explicit heavy-slam record is available and remains separate from normal slam and ordinary heavy attack.
Any direct and radial components must remain separately traceable.
ambiguous_family: A melee attack record exists, but its label or structure does not identify a reliable attack family.
Preserve the record without assigning it to a family by elimination.
undetermined: The weapon is identified as melee, but the supplied attack records do not reliably establish its represented families.

direct_and_radial_slam_components: Associate both components with the corresponding slam family while preserving separate damage-delivery profiles.
Do not add their values or assume identical critical, status, falloff, or target behavior.
stance_evidence_absent: Do not infer normal-attack sequences, arcs, hit counts, movement, multipliers, or forced status effects.
default_or_inherited_attack_values: Do not treat generic, duplicated, inherited, or fallback values as proof of a meaningful distinct attack family without supporting record evidence.
multiple_attack_families_present: Do not select a dominant family from record presence alone.

exceptions: Do not infer stance behavior from weapon class.
Do not infer reliable multi-target coverage from melee classification, range, or follow-through.
Do not classify a melee weapon as normal-attack, heavy-attack, slide, or slam focused solely because that family is represented.
Do not merge statistics across normal attack, heavy attack, slide attack, slam, or heavy slam families.
Do not infer combo generation, consumption, or efficiency from attack-family records.
